text: Avoiding Death on the Yellow Brick Road: Why The App Layer Isn't Dead > AI labs like OpenAI and Anthropic are dominating the "Yellow Brick Road" of horizontal AI tools like coding and writing assistants because they own the model, distribution, and because these problems improve with raw model capability. > There is opportunity for startups in "the rest of Oz": complex, vertical-specific workflows where value comes from deep domain knowledge, multi-step integrations, compliance expertise, and proprietary data flywheels that labs structurally cannot replicate. > "The rest of Oz can win if they own the system of work, the surface where the work of the company actually executes and the data that flows from it gets captured." text: Steven Spielberg on AI Usage in Filmmaking "I don't believe there is any substitute for the soul. I don't think that's an algorithm that is inventible" > "If AI wants to help me find locations, that's great. Saves us some leg work. But don't tell me I don't have the right antagonist in this story, don't tell me how to write my dialogue, don't tell me where the camera has to go, and also don't tell me what a set should look like" > "Use AI as a tool, but do not use AI as the final word on anything creative." text: Vitalik Points to DeepSeek V4 as Local AI Meets Ethereum Privacy Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin updated his progress on local AI, saying DeepSeek V4 now has a 2-bit quantized version that can run within about 90 GB of VRAM, reaching roughly 35 tokens per second on Apple hardware and about 7 tokens per second on AMD hardware. Vitalik said true "CROPS AI" should support multiple hardware platforms, rather than being framed only as "decentralized AI." He added that a "CROPS Ethereum access layer" overlaps with CROPS AI, including ZK-based paid remote LLM calls and private Ethereum RPC reads, and called for more Ethereum-tuned AI models to improve smart contract and protocol-code security. — link

text: Avoiding Death on the Yellow Brick Road - Why The App Layer Isn't Dead Mainstream AI labs (OpenAI and Anthropic) will dominate horizontal, single-step AI tasks (writing and coding) because they own the underlying models, benefit from massive distribution, and continuously improve through raw compute power Startups can thrive by building deeply integrated, vertical "systems of work" that handle complex, multi-step industry workflows requiring strict compliance, custom guardrails, and industry-specific tribal knowledge that generic foundational models cannot replicate

text: Highlight Clip Arthur Hayes: Most Tokens Fall Because Projects Pocket Protocol Revenue On May 23, 2026, BitMEX co-founder Arthur Hayes stated on the What Bitcoin Did podcast that most crypto projects fail to return the economic value created at the protocol level to token holders. He believes early venture capitalists inevitably dump tokens on the market to maximize profits and fulfill fiduciary duties, leading to a continuous decline in token prices. Using Hyperliquid's revenue buyback mechanism as an example, he expressed that current crypto investors have matured and no longer buy into simple white papers or early investor lineups, focusing instead on the actual cash flows token holders can obtain. — link
